events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse scenario. Heavy industrial, Concrete & Masonry, Finishing & curing, rough-complete. Observed field condition: sawcut joints late (random cracking - FD). Need identify component/condition and visible defect cues. Need decision fail because non-compliant/defective. Risk? Random cracking due to late sawcutting can lead to uncontrolled cracks, durability, structural? Finishing/curing. Likely medium or high. Since industrial slab on grade, late sawcut joints cause random cracking; not life-threatening but non-compliant and may require repair. Risk medium maybe high due to potential slab integrity/joint function. Need component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 75? value not asked 0. sound_source etc default 0/empty. findings: random cracking in concrete slab due to delayed sawcutting of control joints; cracks not at planned joint locations. actions: evaluate crack width/depth, repair per engineer, install proper joints timely in future, review sawcut schedule. rationale: ACI 302/308 require control joints sawcut in time to avoid random drying/shrinkage cracking; late sawcutting caused random cracks. references: ACI 302.1R, ACI 308.1.   "rationale": "ACI 302 and ACI 308 require control joints to be sawcut early enough to intercept natural shrinkage cracks. Sawcutting joints late allowed random cracking to form before the joints could act, resulting in uncontrolled cracks and a defective finished slab condition.",
